home.tagline: Join the one-day AI-augmented coding hackathon. The theme stays sealed until the opening ceremony.

theme.title: Hackathon Theme
theme.lockedDesc: To keep the playing field level, the challenge statement will only be revealed at the opening ceremony on hackathon day. Come rested, curious, and ready to build — no pre-work, no prepared projects.

criteria.title: Judging Criteria
criteria.subtitle: Judges score each team from 0 to 15 in every category

ui_ux.name: UI/UX
ui_ux.desc: Visual design, clarity, layout, accessibility and overall user experience.

frontend.name: Frontend
frontend.desc: Code quality, responsiveness across devices, performance and interactivity.

backend.name: Backend
backend.desc: Architecture, API design, data handling, security and reliability.

innovation.name: Innovation
innovation.desc: Originality of the idea, creativity, and how well it addresses a real-world problem.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Build real projects with Claude as your pair programmer
howItWorks.intro: This is an AI-augmented hackathon. Every team receives an API key that lets you use Claude — Anthropic's AI model — to help you build. You will be judged on what you ship, not on writing every line yourself. The goal is to combine your technical skills with Claude to create something useful under the theme revealed on the day.

claude.title: Claude
claude.desc: Claude is an AI assistant you can chat with, ask to write code, debug issues, explain unfamiliar tech, and review your work.

apiKey.title: Your API Key
apiKey.desc: When the admin reveals keys, you'll find yours in the Team tab. Copy it and use it with Claude Code or any API client to make Claude requests.

claudeCode.title: Claude Code
claudeCode.desc: A CLI tool that plugs Claude directly into your terminal and editor. It reads your project, edits files, runs commands, and ships features while you guide it.

video.title: Watch: Install Claude Code in VS Code
video.subtitle: A quick walkthrough — set it up before hackathon day

howItWorks.note: You don't need to be an AI expert. Describe what you want, show Claude the code, let it help — then iterate. Your technical skills + Claude's speed = a working project in a few hours.

usage.title: How to use your API key
usage.subtitle: Quick setup — pick your operating system

steps.title: Copy your key from the Team tab
steps.desc: Once the admin reveals keys, open the Team tab on this site. You'll see your team's API key in a gold card. Tap the copy button.

steps.title: Install Claude Code globally
steps.desc: Claude Code is a CLI that plugs Claude into your terminal and editor. It needs Node.js 18 or higher. Open a terminal and run:

steps.title: Set your API key as an environment variable
steps.desc: Paste your key after the equals sign. This exposes the key to Claude Code so it can authenticate with Anthropic.

steps.title: Open your project and launch Claude
steps.desc: In the terminal, cd into your project folder (or open it in VS Code / Cursor) and run 'claude'. Describe what you want — Claude will read your files, edit them and run commands for you.

login.subtitle: Login or create a new team account
login.registrationClosedBanner: Registration is closed. Existing teams and judges can still sign in.
login.emailHint: Please enter your university or organization email (e.g. name@university.edu). Personal emails like Gmail, Yahoo, and Hotmail are not accepted.
login.regCompleteDesc: Welcome to Code For Hebron Hackathon 2026
login.saveQrNote: Save this QR code. Judges will scan it to award points.

errors.teamNameNoSpaces: Team name cannot contain spaces — it's used as your login username
errors.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 6 characters
errors.memberRequired: At least one team member is required
errors.memberMinTwo: At least 2 team members are required
errors.memberMaxFour: A team can have at most 4 members
errors.memberPhoneRequired: All members must have a phone number
errors.emailPersonal: Personal emails (Gmail, Yahoo, etc.) are not allowed. Please use your university or organization email.

team.teamMembersDesc: Click on a member to edit their details

raiseHand.notePlaceholder: e.g. stuck on Docker deploy, need the API key, ...

volunteers.title: Manage Volunteers
volunteers.desc: Create accounts for hackathon-day check-in volunteers
volunteers.empty: No volunteers yet — create one to get started

admin.registrationDesc: Control whether new teams can sign up. Existing teams and judges can always log in.

pwReset.title: Reset Password
pwReset.desc: Search for a user and set a new password

admin.freezeDesc: Lock the scoreboard so participants can't see new scores in the final minutes
admin.confirmDeleteTeam: Are you sure you want to delete team "{{name}}"? This will remove all its members and scores.

inviteLink.title: Invite Link
inviteLink.desc: Set the Claude organisation invite link. Teams see it after check-in or when you reveal to all.
inviteLink.note: Teams that a volunteer has checked in can always see the link. This button controls visibility for teams that haven't been checked in yet.

apiKeys.title: Invite Links
apiKeys.desc: Upload a CSV file with invite links — each team gets one link. Links are revealed per-team when a volunteer confirms their arrival.
apiKeys.confirmClear: Are you sure you want to clear all API keys?
